qualifying_org_definition: Not specified in state policy; the manual (MS 2100.A.3) permits any 'unpaid (volunteer) work' toward the 80-hour monthly requirement without restricting the type or tax status of the organization. No state-approved site list or MOU requirement found for the general volunteer lane (the FSET-241 Workfare Training Site Agreement applies only to the separate E&T/Workfare lane).
verification_instrument: Written documentation from the organization (letter or statement confirming hours); no named Kentucky state form identified. Per MS 2100.A.3, the caseworker must verify hours of in-kind/unpaid work at recertification; client statement is acceptable if not questionable. Uploaded to Citizen Connect (kynect portal).
hours_formula_detail: MS 2100.A.3 states explicitly: 'Be working 80 hours per month.' State follows federal default under 7 CFR 273.24. Hours are systematically converted to determine 80+/month threshold (MS 2100.D.2).
accepts_outofstate_detail: Kentucky policy (MS 2100.A.3) refers only to 'unpaid (volunteer) work' with no restriction on the geographic location or registration state of the sponsoring organization. No in-state requirement, approved-site list, or MOU requirement was found in the Operation Manual or E&T State Plans for the general volunteer lane. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit or public institution is acceptable absent an explicit state restriction — so out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: Five rural counties retain ABAWD waivers effective December 1, 2025 (through at least FY2026): Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, and Wolfe counties. All remaining 115 counties are fully enforced as of November 1, 2025, when the prior statewide 117-county waiver expired.
submission_deadline: Hours of in-kind/unpaid work must be verified at each recertification for all prior months in the certification period (MS 2100.A.3). Changes in work hours dropping below 20 hrs/week must be reported within 10 days. No separate monthly submission deadline; documentation is collected and reviewed at recertification.
notes:
Kentucky Operation Manual Volume IIA (OMTL-683, R. 10/25/25) MS 2100.A.3 explicitly allows compliance via 'in-kind work, or unpaid (volunteer) work, or any combination' for 80 hours/month, with no required EDP, county assignment, or MOU — those apply only to the separately opt-in E&T/Workfare lane (MS 2500, 2510). Hours of in-kind/unpaid work must be verified at recertification but no specific Kentucky state form is named; no form number was found. ABAWD requirements are enforced statewide as of 11/1/2025 with five rural counties retaining a waiver exemption.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial research across 10+ searches and 8 URL fetches targeting hidden presteps, EDP requirements, approved site lists, MOU requirements, and the FY2025/FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plans. Key findings: (1) Kentucky's SNAP E&T is confirmed 'all-voluntary' — participants are not mandated into E&T, so no EDP or enrollment prestep exists before volunteer hours count in the general ABAWD lane. (2) No approved volunteer site list found: the only site agreement found (Form FSET-241, Workfare Training Site Agreement) applies exclusively to the E&T/workfare lane, not the general unpaid-work lane. (3) No MOU requirement for nonprofits in the general lane — confirmed by absence of any such requirement in CHFS materials and advocacy-org analysis. (4) Verification instrument resolved from 'unknown' to 'written documentation from organization' — the state manual requires caseworker verification of in-kind hours at recertification, with client statement acceptable if not questionable, and documents uploaded via Citizen Connect (kynect portal); no named state form exists. (5) The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 2025, effective 2025-2026) uses 'qualified nonprofit or public institution' language federally but Kentucky has not imposed additional pre-registration or approval requirements on top of this. (6) The FY2025 and FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan PDFs were retrieved but are FlateDecode-compressed and could not be parsed; however, the FY2025 plan is consistently described in search results as an all-voluntary program. (7) Anti-hunger orgs (Kentucky Center for Economic Policy, Kentucky Voices for Health) document barriers around document upload technology and case management confusion, but neither identifies a mandatory pre-step gating volunteer-hour certification — the barriers are administrative friction, not a structural prestep. Tier 1 classification is confirmed. Confidence raised to 'high' on the basis of multi-source convergence: the state manual (MS 2100.A.3), FY2025 E&T plan description (all-voluntary), absence of any FSET-241-style agreement for the general lane, and consistent reporting from advocacy organizations.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS FORMULA: Confirmed federal_80 — MS 2100.A.3 states 'Be working 80 hours per month' verbatim; MS 2100.D.2 confirms 'hours will be systematically converted in order to determine if the member is working 80 hours or more/month.' No workfare formula used. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: Silent — the manual has no restriction on org location or registration state for the general volunteer lane; no in-state requirement or approved site list applies. Out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted under federal default. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: Five counties retain ABAWD waivers effective Dec 1, 2025: Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, and Wolfe. The prior 117-county statewide waiver expired Nov 30, 2025; all other 115 counties are now fully enforced. Confirmed via web research and search synthesis (FNA waivers page, Ballotpedia, KY Lantern). (4) SUBMISSION UX: Primary mechanism is online_portal (Citizen Connect / kynect portal) confirmed by KY Voices for Health analysis noting document upload is 'the primary way beneficiaries are required to verify compliance'; paper county office also implied by DCBS workflow. Cadence is at_recertification per MS 2100.A.3: 'hours must be verified at each recertification for the prior months in between cert periods.' No separate monthly filing deadline; 10-day rule applies only to reporting changes in hours. (5) FORM FIELDS: No named state form; applied the standard baseline fields. The verification letter from the org must establish the activity, org identity, hours total, and time period to satisfy caseworker review at recertification.
